definition: A procedure eventually halts or reaches a defined terminal state.
A condition that determines when a procedure halts.
A measure that moves closer to completion at each step.
A property that remains true through valid execution steps.
A property preserved before and after each loop iteration.
A property preserved across valid system transitions.
A situation near the boundary of expected operation.
A case that disproves or challenges an expected behavior.
A test targeting empty singleton zero maximum threshold or invalid cases.
